pr.title: Melee decomp: Fighter common action matches
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Exact-match decompilation work for Fighter common actions, with narrow efLib signature support. The PR reports 12 newly exact functions across 7 files, zero slice regressions, and cleanup follow-up for literals, sqrt forms, and an inline-depth pragma replacement.

key_files.role: Main Fighter common action matching work; changed local variable naming/order, Fighter_x1A88 access patterns, physics prediction expressions, sqrt handling, PAD_STACK usage, and several functions including ftCo_800A3908, ftCo_800A4038, ftCo_800A4BEC, ftCo_800A9904, ftCo_800A9CB4, and ftCo_800AC5A0.
Fighter guard matching work; review identified a pragma inline_depth block and author later confirmed use of an inline-wrapper approach for ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim.
Narrow effect-library support: efLib_SetParamAlpha body signature changed from four parameters to two parameters.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: efLib support required because ftCo_Guard.c uses two-argument efLib_SetParamAlpha

key_files.role: Header declaration updated to match the two-argument efLib_SetParamAlpha signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: eflib.h declaration changed to void efLib_SetParamAlpha(HSD_GObj* gobj, u8 alpha)

key_files.role: Fighter action matching work; bot report lists several ftaction new matches and .data match improvement.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftAction_80073354, ftAction_80073118, ftAction_80071FC8, ftAction_80072320, and .data improvements

key_files.role: Fighter dynamics matching work; bot report lists ftCo_8009DD94 exact and several unmatched improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftdynamics matches/improvements

key_files.role: Small Fighter common ThrownKirby matching cleanup; bot report lists ftCo_800BE6AC reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_800BE6AC 97.68% to 100.00%

what_changed: PR body states this was exact-match decompilation for Fighter common actions plus efLib signature support required by the matches.

Changed seven files: src/melee/ef/eflib.c, src/melee/ef/eflib.h, src/melee/ft/ftaction.c, src/melee/ft/ftdynamics.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c, src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c, and src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKirby.c.
efLib_SetParamAlpha was narrowed from a four-argument form with unused float parameters to a two-argument form in both source and header.

ftCo_0A01.c work included declaration-order changes, replacing temp-style locals with role names such as data, x_time, terminal_time, predicted_y, ceiling_pos, ceiling_normal, ceiling_line_id, and ceiling_flags, and using struct Fighter_x1A88_t* data for repeated fp->x1A88 access.

ftCo_0A01.c diff shows codegen-sensitive forms including PAD_STACK, scoped floor_result temporaries around mpCheckFloor, altered out-of-bounds control flow, and retained manual __frsqrte-based sqrt sequence for ftCo_800A9CB4.
Review cleanup removed or avoided address-named literal extern/static constants where reviewers requested literals, except for a documented ftCo_800A9CB4 sqrt code-shape exception.
Author reported isolated slice verification: 12 new matches, 0 broken matches, 0 fuzzy regressions, 0 metric regressions; full ship-set report: 96 new matches and no regressions.

smart_moves.text: Kept efLib signature support narrow and tied it to a compiling FT callsite need rather than broad effect-library churn.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: efLib.c and eflib.h included because ftCo_Guard.c now uses two-argument efLib_SetParamAlpha and slice failed to compile without support pair

smart_moves.text: Responded to review by expanding generated-looking literal externs and inlining a one-off 120.0f constant use as a literal expression.
evidence_refs: review_comments: r3408643258 'You can expand these into literals'

smart_moves.text: Used direct sqrtf cleanup where it preserved the match, specifically reporting ftCo_800AC5A0 remained cleaned up to use sqrtf.
evidence_refs: review_comments: r3410067739 'ditto, repeat across the whole files'

smart_moves.text: Documented a narrow exception for ftCo_800A9CB4: direct sqrtf was tested, regressed that function from 100.00% to 96.54%, and only the local manual sqrt sequence was reverted.
Replaced a broad inline-depth pragma approach in ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim with an inline-wrapper approach after review.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated address-named float externs or one-off static constants should be challenged and expanded back into literals when the PR evidence does not justify data ownership.

Prefer direct sqrtf for recognizable sqrt sequences, but a local handwritten __frsqrte refinement sequence can be retained when direct sqrtf has explicit objdiff/bot evidence of regressing an exact match.
Pragma-based inlining should be avoided or removed when an inline-wrapper approach can provide the needed helper-body shape.
When recovering codegen-sensitive fighter functions, declaration order, temporary scoping, and local names may be part of the match surface; keep such choices tied to narrow verification evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800A3908, ftCo_800A4038, ftCo_800A4BEC, ftCo_800A9904, ftCo_800A9CB4 hunks

naming_conventions.convention: Use short role names for clear object roles: data for &fp->x1A88, cur_fp for current fighter, ip for item pointer, gobj/jobj where applicable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct Fighter_x1A88_t* data = &fp->x1A88 in ftCo_0A01.c

naming_conventions.convention: Replace temp_rXX and generic var_f names with local role names when the computation is clear, such as x_time, terminal_time, predicted_y, y_pos, ceiling_pos, ceiling_normal, ceiling_line_id, and ceiling_flags.
Do not promote address-style constants like ftCo_804D8914 or ftCo_804D87D8-family externs when review and cleanup show literal expressions are sufficient.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in ftCo_0A01.c for codegen-sensitive functions, including ftCo_800A4038 and ftCo_800A9CB4, with PR-level verification claiming no regressions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: PAD_STACK is a matching tactic and should remain narrow and evidence-backed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Manual __frsqrte plus three Newton refinement steps and volatile storage was retained for ftCo_800A9CB4 after direct sqrtf regressed the function from 100.00% to 96.54%.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Reviewers initially identified this as sqrtf; the exception is supported only for the cited ftCo_800A9CB4 callsites.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped temporaries around mpCheckFloor, such as floor_result assigned to result, were used to influence local codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence shows the shape but does not provide a standalone explanation beyond the overall match verification.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline-wrapper approach was preferred over #pragma inline_depth for ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim after review.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The exact final wrapper code is not fully visible in the loaded excerpt.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_comments: r3410801079 and r3410801107 document ftCo_800A9CB4 exception

review_feedback.feedback: Remove #pragma inline_depth block, possibly replacing it with inline-wrapper trick.

notes: Loaded raw_diff and review_comments are truncated, so this intake avoids claiming complete final code details for all hunks.
Verification claims are from PR body/bot excerpts; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.

The PR body says 12 newly exact functions across 7 files, while an earlier bot excerpt lists 14 new matches including data items; this record preserves the PR body's 12-function claim and notes bot-listed examples without reconciling the count.
The inline-wrapper final implementation for ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim is asserted by the author comment but not fully visible in loaded diff excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep literals inline unless data ownership is evidenced
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2684, reviewers asked to expand address-named float externs and inline a one-off static const 120.0f. The author reported resolving both by using literal expressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: sqrtf cleanup is preferred, but exact-match exceptions need regression evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: Review identified manual sqrt sequences as sqrtf. The author kept ftCo_800AC5A0 cleaned up to sqrtf, but retained ftCo_800A9CB4's local __frsqrte refinement sequence after direct sqrtf regressed that function from 100.00% to 96.54%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use inline wrappers before pragma inline_depth for local match shaping
accepted_candidate_records.text: For ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim, review requested removing a #pragma inline_depth block and suggested an inline-wrapper trick; the author later confirmed the inline-wrapper approach was used.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow sqrtf exception pattern with explicit regression evidence
source_update_candidates.text: Review should prefer sqrtf for recognizable __frsqrte refinement sequences, but a local handwritten sqrt sequence may be allowed when direct sqrtf is tested and causes a documented exact-match regression for the specific function. PR #2684 provides an example: ftCo_800A9CB4 dropped from 100.00% to 96.54% when changed to sqrtf, while ftCo_800AC5A0 stayed cleaned up to sqrtf.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is compatible with existing standards on canonical cleanup and evidence-backed tactics, but curator should decide whether the sqrt-specific exception belongs in global standards.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer inline-wrapper matching over broad inline_depth pragmas
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2684 review requested removing an inline_depth pragma block in ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim and suggested an inline-wrapper trick; the author confirmed switching to the inline-wrapper approach.
source_update_candidates.reason: May refine the existing avoid-pragmas-register-asm standard with a concrete preferred alternative.

source_update_candidates.title: efLib_SetParamAlpha signature support for Fighter guard callsites
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2684, src/melee/ef/eflib.c and src/melee/ef/eflib.h were changed so efLib_SetParamAlpha takes HSD_GObj* and u8 alpha only; PR body says this was required because ftCo_Guard.c uses the two-argument form and the slice failed to compile without the support pair.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-level fact about an effect helper signature dependency; curator should verify against current source before promoting.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that all __frsqrte sequences should remain manual; reviewers preferred sqrtf and only ftCo_800A9CB4 has documented regression evidence in this PR.
Do not infer final source details for truncated ftaction.c, ftdynamics.c, ftCo_Guard.c, or ftCo_ThrownKirby.c hunks beyond changed-file metadata and bot/PR summaries.
Do not treat PAD_STACK or volatile sqrt storage as normal style; the PR evidence only supports narrow use under match verification.
Do not promote the PR body's verification as independently validated by this intake run.
